ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM covers raw lamb meat broadly; no shank-specific sub-code exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent match used for the lean raw shank preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept specifically for lamb shank confirmed in public SNOMED CT browser; code omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

preparation_method: trimmed of external and internal separable fat

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw bone-in lamb shank weighs approximately 300 g, yielding roughly 180–200 g of lean cooked meat after bone removal and cooking losses.

nutrient_source.title: Lamb, New Zealand, imported, shank, separable lean only, raw
nutrient_source.organization: Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from Singapore HPB Foodo database (data_source: hpb, confidence 0.95) for this exact food item F06050021. Cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for lamb shank, separable lean only, raw (FDC ID 174351) to confirm plausibility. All HPB values are within expected range of USDA reference.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for lean raw lamb shank as carbohydrate content is 0.0 g per 100 g (confirmed zero — pure muscle meat with no carbohydrate fraction). No GI value is assigned. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (110 mg/100 g) with high potassium (350 mg/100 g) yielding a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Lean lamb shank provides a sodium-to-potassium ratio well below 1:1, which supports blood pressure regulation; patients with hypertension should prepare it without added salt or high-sodium braising liquids and pair it with potassium-rich vegetables.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein (23.9 g/100 g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0.0 g carbohydrate and 0.0 g sugar per 100 g, lean raw lamb shank has no glycaemic impact and is suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes when prepared without sugar-containing glazes or sweet braising sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (1.9 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (~0.8 g/100 g) after full fat trimming
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean lamb shank with all separable fat removed contains minimal saturated fat and cholesterol (71 mg/100 g), making it an acceptable lean red meat choice for cardiovascular disease patients when consumed in moderate portions and prepared by grilling, roasting, or braising without added saturated fats.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Arachidonic acid (omega-6) content in red meat may amplify inflammatory mediators in RA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lean lamb shank is red meat containing arachidonic acid that can amplify inflammatory mediators in rheumatoid arthritis; consume only occasionally, prioritise fatty fish or plant-based proteins, and if consumed pair with anti-inflammatory spices such as turmeric or ginger.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lamb shank (raw, lean) is the lower leg portion of a lamb carcass, comprising dense, deep-red skeletal muscle meat with the bone retained. All external and internal separable fat has been removed, leaving only lean muscle tissue. In its raw state the meat has a firm, dense texture and a mild gamey aroma characteristic of young sheep. It is widely used in slow-braised preparations such as Moroccan tagine, Irish stew, and Middle Eastern lamb dishes, where the collagen-rich connective tissue around the shank bone breaks down into gelatin during prolonged moist-heat cooking, producing a rich, tender result. Nutritionally, lean raw lamb shank is an excellent source of complete protein (23.9 g/100 g), providing all essential amino acids. Total fat is very low at 1.9 g/100 g with saturated fat approximately 0.8 g/100 g after trimming. Carbohydrate content is zero, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. Sodium is moderate at 110 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 350 mg/100 g, supporting a favourable blood pressure profile. Iron content is 1.9 mg/100 g (haem iron, highly bioavailable). Cholesterol is 71 mg/100 g. The food is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is classified as a raw, minimally processed red meat and is associated with mamak and Middle Eastern culinary traditions in the Singapore food context.

Is lamb shank good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from Singapore HPB Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for the exact food item F06050021 (Lamb shank, raw, lean). Values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy lamb shank lean raw entry and found to be within expected range. No estimation or analogy required for core macros.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero — lean raw lamb shank is a pure muscle meat product containing no carbohydrate fraction; this is naturally zero and consistent with all approved reference sources. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal muscle food with no plant fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source; ruminant trans fats such as vaccenic acid are present at trace levels below reporting threshold in lean trimmed cuts). Saturated_fat_g (0.8 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.7 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.2 g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174351 (lamb shank, separable lean only, raw) as HPB source does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions; total fat 1.9 g is consistent. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific HPB entry (F06050021) are not disaggregated in the HPB Foodo public dataset, and cross-mapping to USDA SR Legacy micronutrient values was not performed to avoid unsanctioned source mixing: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Sources consulted and confirmed as not providing disaggregated values for this exact entry: Singapore HPB Foodo (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/), accessed 2026-04-16. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); glycemic_index set to null and gi_category set to not_applicable per critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods. Primary cuisine tagged as mamak per input cultural metadata, reflecting common use of lamb shank in Singapore mamak restaurant preparations. Food subgroup listed as Mutton in source input; retained as Lamb / Mutton to reflect accurate species classification (lamb = sheep under 12 months).

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English name 'Lamb shank, raw, lean'. No local-language names are mandatory as this is a generic international ingredient, not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish requiring mandatory multilingual coverage under G6.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 114.0, protein_g 23.9, fat_g 1.9, carbohydrate_g 0.0 all present and numeric. Carbohydrate_g zero is justified (naturally zero, pure muscle meat). Source is Singapore HPB Foodo (approved primary regional source) at confidence 0.95, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.0 (naturally zero, animal muscle food), sodium_mg 110.0, saturated_fat_g 0.8 (estimated from USDA SR Legacy, documented in notes), sugar_g 0.0 (naturally zero) — all present as numeric values and plausible.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate food per critical rule — setting to 0 would fail q4), gi_category 'not_applicable', glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0, gi_source present with full explanation. Food has 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g; GI is not applicable.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for lamb shank (exact match). FoodEx2 code A0CKM (lamb meat, raw) used as closest_parent match with gap note explaining no shank-specific sub-code exists. SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, human_condition_name correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes'), Cardiovascular Disease (low concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 6 entries covering canonical name, alternate English forms, and regional variants. aliases_by_language english array mirrors all 6 entries verbatim. All strings in aliases_by_language.english verified to exist verbatim in flat aliases array. No mandatory multilingual aliases required as food is a generic international ingredient.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed from input JSON with HPB source values and USDA SR Legacy cross-reference.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type to 'closest_parent'; confirmed glycemic_index is null and gi_category is 'not_applicable'; added partial -1 documentation to notes.

Iteration 3: Expanded notes field to fully document all 15 per_100g fields set to -1 (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) with explicit reason (HPB Foodo does not disaggregate these micronutrient values for this entry) and source consulted (HPB Foodo, accessed 2026-04-16), satisfying G3 numeric hygiene requirement.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all 6 hard gates and scores 8/8. All 15 per_100g fields set to -1 are now fully documented in the notes field with explicit reasons for unavailability and the source consulted that confirmed unavailability, resolving the G3 numeric hygiene failure from iteration 2. Glycemic_index correctly set to null (not 0) with gi_category 'not_applicable' for this zero-carbohydrate food, resolving the Q4 failure from iteration 2. All core macros present and numeric with zero carbohydrate justified as naturally zero for pure muscle meat. Ontology uses real FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes with honest gap notes. All three required health conditions present with clinical guidance including Type 2 Diabetes. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Aliases consistent between flat array and structured object.
